platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems describe graphs in text and ask for numeric or short text answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure could mean the student can't read bar graphs, can't interpret picture graph keys, or can't handle scaled data — different remediation paths.

bar-graphs-picture-graphs.notes: Separate picture-graphs (G1) and bar-graphs-read (G2) topics already exist. This G3 topic is the scaled/combined version. Consider whether this should focus only on scaled graphs since the basics are covered by lower-grade topics.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test reading/interpreting line plots with fractional measurements. Requires fraction addition as a prerequisite, but the assessed skill is line plot interpretation.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Combines fraction arithmetic with data interpretation — distinct from pure fraction operations or basic line plots.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Line plot data is described in text; all answers are numeric.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to work with fractional data in a visual context.

line-plots.notes: Note: there is a duplicate topic line-plots-fractions that appears to overlap significantly. See merge recommendation there.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems span length, mass, and liquid volume conversions across both customary and metric systems. This is very broad.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Conversion requires multiplying/dividing by unit factors — different from raw measurement.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems are text-based arithmetic.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure could stem from not knowing conversion factors, or computational errors, or confusion between systems. Too broad to pinpoint.

unit-conversion.notes: This topic has many specific children (convert-length-smaller, convert-mass, convert-time, convert-volume, etc.) that cover individual conversions. This works as a capstone topic that tests fluency across all conversion types. Acceptable given the children exist.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test computing mean, median, and/or mode. While these are three measures, they are taught together and represent one conceptual unit of 'measures of center.'

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires sorting data, summing and dividing (mean), finding middle value (median), and counting frequencies (mode) — distinct from graph reading or raw computation.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems are text-based with numeric answers.

grade_boundary_natural.reasoning: Grade 5 per CCSS 6.SP.B.5c (introduced in 5th in many curricula).

remediation_useful.reasoning: Problems separately test each measure, so diagnostic can identify which specific measure is problematic.

mean-median-mode.notes: Could potentially split mean vs. median vs. mode, but they are conventionally taught as a unit and the problem bank can diagnose which sub-skill is weak.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems involve inch measurements — comparing, adding, subtracting lengths in inches. Focused skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Applies addition/subtraction specifically to inch measurements, building comfort with the customary system.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems give measurements as numbers and ask for numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure pinpoints discomfort with inch-based measurement specifically.

measure-length-inches.notes: Some hard problems creep into perimeter territory, which could be cleaned up.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems involve centimeter measurements — comparing, adding, subtracting lengths in cm. Parallel to measure-length-inches.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Same measurement skills as inches but in metric, which is a separate skill for students learning both systems.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems text-based with numeric answers in cm.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure pinpoints discomfort with metric measurement specifically.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test measuring to half and quarter inch — a specific precision skill beyond whole inches.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires understanding fractional ruler markings, which is qualitatively different from whole-inch measurement.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Problems describe ruler positions in text; answers are fractional measurements.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to work with fractional ruler markings — a specific gap.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test comparing weights, simple weight arithmetic, and basic unit awareness (pounds, ounces). Consistent skill set for intro level.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Weight comparison and estimation use different intuition than length measurement.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems text-based with numeric or comparison answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies lack of weight/mass conceptual understanding.

weight-mass-intro.notes: Broader scope acceptable for Grade 2 introduction.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test comparing liquid volumes and simple capacity arithmetic. Consistent intro-level skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Liquid volume comparison/estimation is distinct from weight or length.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies lack of capacity/volume conceptual understanding.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test reading analog clocks to 5-minute intervals. A student could pass this but fail minute-level reading.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires skip-counting by 5s around the clock face — a step up from hour/half-hour but simpler than arbitrary minutes.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Text descriptions of clock hand positions with time answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure pinpoints inability to read 5-minute intervals on a clock.

tell-time-5-minutes.notes: Hard problems include elapsed time, which should be cleaned from this bank.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test calculating time differences. Distinct from simply reading a clock.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires subtracting across hours and minutes, sometimes crossing noon — different from clock reading.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with time-duration answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure specifically identifies inability to compute elapsed time, guiding focused remediation.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test reading picture graphs with keys. A student could pass this but fail bar graph reading.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires understanding picture-to-number mapping with single and multi-unit keys.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Graph data described in text; numeric or comparison answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to interpret picture-based data representations.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test reading and comparing values from bar graphs. Distinct from creating graphs.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Reading bar heights and computing differences — distinct from picture graph key interpretation.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Graph data described in text; numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure pinpoints inability to extract and compare data from bar graphs.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test choosing scales, determining bar heights, and labeling — all creation-focused. Distinct from reading graphs.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Creating a graph requires choosing appropriate scales and translating data to visual representation — higher-order than reading.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Problems ask about scale choices and bar heights numerically, not actual drawing. Platform-compatible.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student can't translate data into graphical form.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test reading/interpreting line plots with whole numbers. Distinct from fractional line plots.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Interpreting X marks above a number line and computing totals/frequencies — different from bar or picture graphs.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Line plot data described in text; numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to read line plot representations.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test reading/creating line plots with fractional data — consistent skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: This topic is nearly identical to the 'line-plots' topic (also G5, also fractional data). The descriptions differ slightly but problems overlap substantially.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Text-based with numeric/fractional answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure would pinpoint the same gap as the line-plots topic.

line-plots-fractions.mergeRecommendation: Merge with 'line-plots' (Line Plots with Fractions, id: line-plots). These two topics cover the same G5 standard (5.MD.B.2) with nearly identical problem types. Keep one topic.
line-plots-fractions.notes: Duplicate topic. The 'line-plots' topic has 20 problems and this has 15 — merge into one with the best problems from each.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test knowing and applying relationships among inches, feet, yards, and miles. Focused on customary length relationships.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires memorizing and applying customary conversion factors — different from metric or from doing multi-step conversions.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

grade_boundary_natural.reasoning: Grade 3, bridging to Grade 4 conversion topics.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't know customary length relationships.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test knowing metric length relationships (mm, cm, m, km). Focused skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Metric system is base-10 which requires different reasoning than customary conversions.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't know metric length relationships.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test knowing mass unit relationships and simple conversions (oz/lb, g/kg).

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Mass units have different conversion factors than length or volume — distinct knowledge.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't know mass unit relationships.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test converting between time units (seconds, minutes, hours). Focused skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Time conversions use base-60, which is different from base-10 metric or customary conversions.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't know time unit relationships.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test knowing liquid volume unit relationships (cups, pints, quarts, gallons, mL, L).

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Liquid volume has its own unit hierarchy distinct from length, mass, or time.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't know liquid volume relationships.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems convert larger length units to smaller ones (feet to inches, yards to feet). One direction of conversion.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Converting to smaller units requires multiplication — distinct from converting to larger units (division).

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to multiply by conversion factors for length.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems convert smaller length units to larger ones (inches to feet, cm to meters). Inverse of convert-length-smaller.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Converting to larger units requires division, often yielding remainders or fractions — more complex than multiply-down.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to divide by conversion factors for length.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems convert mass units (lb to oz, kg to g, and reverse). Focused on mass conversions.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Mass conversion factors (16 oz/lb, 1000 g/kg) are different from length or volume.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to perform mass conversions.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems convert between time units (hours to minutes, minutes to seconds, etc.).

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Base-60 conversions are distinct from base-10 or base-16 conversions in other measurement types.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to perform time unit conversions.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems convert liquid volume units (cups to pints, quarts to gallons, mL to L).

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Volume conversion factors are unique to this measurement type.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to perform liquid volume conversions.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test comparing units across measurement systems (e.g., is a meter longer than a yard?). Focused cross-system skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires approximate knowledge of cross-system equivalences — different from within-system conversion.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with comparison or numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies lack of cross-system measurement intuition.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test computing change from a purchase — a specific money skill beyond just counting coins.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Making change requires subtraction from a payment amount, often requiring counting up strategy — different from just counting coins.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with monetary answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to compute change, a practical and specific gap.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test applying money skills in word problem contexts — addition, subtraction, and comparison with dollars and cents.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Word problem interpretation layer on top of money arithmetic — distinct from just counting coins or making change.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with monetary answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to apply money concepts in context.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test reading and creating data tables — a distinct data representation from graphs.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Organizing data into rows/columns and extracting information requires tabular reasoning distinct from graph interpretation.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Tables can be described in text; answers are numeric.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to work with tabular data.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test understanding arrays as repeated addition leading to multiplication. A student could pass this but fail single-digit multiplication facts.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Arrays are a visual/spatial model for multiplication — distinct from equal groups or fact recall.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All problems describe arrays in text with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't understand the array model for multiplication.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test multiplication fact recall up to 10x10. Pure procedural fluency.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Fact recall/fluency is distinct from conceptual understanding (arrays, equal groups) or multi-digit algorithms.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies lack of multiplication fact fluency — a critical and specific gap.

multiply-within-100.notes: This is the capstone fact-fluency topic. The granular fact-family topics (by-2-5-10, by-3-4, by-6-7-8-9) feed into it.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test translating word problem contexts into multiplication and computing. Distinct from raw fact recall.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires identifying the multiplication structure in context (equal groups, arrays, comparison) — an application-level skill.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to apply multiplication in context, distinct from fact fluency issues.

multiply-word-problems.notes: The more specific word problem topics (equal-groups, comparison, area) exist as children/extensions.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems mix commutative, associative, and distributive properties — three distinct concepts. A student could understand commutativity but not distributivity.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Understanding properties is conceptual/reasoning, distinct from fact recall or word problems.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric or yes/no answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure could mean confusion about any of three different properties — hard to pinpoint which one.

properties-of-multiplication.notes: The commutative-property-mult and associative-distributive-mult topics already exist as more granular alternatives. This topic may function as a combined assessment. Review whether to deprecate in favor of the granular topics.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test multiplying up to 4-digit by 1-digit and 2-digit by 2-digit numbers. Consistent procedural skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Multi-digit multiplication requires managing place value and regrouping — distinct from single-digit facts.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All numeric computation with text answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to execute multi-digit multiplication algorithm.

multi-digit-multiply.notes: Capstone topic with granular children (3x1, 4x1, 2x2 area model, 2x2 standard) handling specific sub-skills.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test fluent multi-digit multiplication including 3-digit by 2-digit and larger. Extension of multi-digit-multiply.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Larger numbers require sustained attention to place value across more partial products — a fluency extension.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All numeric computation.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that while basic multi-digit multiplication may be understood, fluency with larger numbers is lacking.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems mix estimating products (multiplication) and estimating quotients (division). A student could estimate products well but struggle with quotient estimation.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Estimating a product (round then multiply) vs. estimating a quotient (find compatible numbers then divide) are different strategies.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure could mean inability to estimate products, quotients, or both — doesn't pinpoint the gap.

estimation-multiplication-division.splitRecommendation: However, separate estimate-products (estimation-multiplication) and estimate-quotients (estimation-division) topics already exist. This combined topic should be deprecated or kept only as a mixed-practice capstone. Recommend removing it in favor of the two existing specific topics.
estimation-multiplication-division.notes: Redundant with estimation-multiplication and estimation-division. Recommend deprecation.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test understanding multiplication as combining equal groups. Distinct from arrays or fact recall.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Equal groups is a concrete real-world model — bags of apples, groups of students — different reasoning than arrays.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't understand the equal-groups model for multiplication.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test multiplication by 2, 5, and 10 — the anchor facts. A student could pass this but fail 6-9 facts.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: These facts connect to skip-counting patterns (2s, 5s, 10s) — a different strategy than deriving 7x8 from known facts.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that anchor multiplication facts are not yet automatic.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test identity property (x*1=x) and zero property (x*0=0). Focused conceptual topic.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Understanding why anything times 0 is 0 and anything times 1 is itself is conceptual — different from fact drill.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric or true/false answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies misconception about identity/zero properties specifically.

multiply-by-0-1.notes: Small topic but the conceptual understanding it targets is specific and important.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test 3s and 4s multiplication facts. A student could pass 2-5-10 facts but fail 3-4 facts.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: 3s and 4s require different strategies (doubling for 4s, counting pattern for 3s) than anchor facts.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure specifically identifies which fact families need work.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test the harder single-digit facts (6-9 times tables). These are the facts students typically struggle with most.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: 6-9 facts require derivation strategies (e.g., 6x7 = 6x5 + 6x2) rather than simple skip-counting.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies the hardest fact families as the specific gap.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test rapid single-digit multiplication. But this is indistinguishable from multiply-within-100.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: The problems are identical in type to multiply-within-100. Both test fluent multiplication of single-digit numbers.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Same remediation target as multiply-within-100.

multiply-fluency-100.mergeRecommendation: Merge with 'multiply-within-100'. Both topics test the same skill (fluent single-digit multiplication) at the same grade level. The 'fluency' framing adds no diagnostic value over the existing topic.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test the place-value pattern of multiplying by powers of 10. Focused and distinct skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Understanding the 'append zeros' pattern requires place-value reasoning, not fact memorization.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies a specific place-value misconception about powers of 10.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test multiplying multiples of 10 (30x40, 60x70). Extends the powers-of-10 pattern.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires combining basic facts with place-value reasoning (3x4=12, so 30x40=1200) — a specific bridging skill.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to extend fact knowledge via place value.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test 2-digit by 1-digit multiplication without carrying. A student could pass this but fail when regrouping is needed.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: No-regroup multiplication isolates the place-value decomposition step without the added complexity of carrying.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that the student doesn't understand place-value decomposition for multiplication, independent of regrouping.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test 2-digit by 1-digit with regrouping. Isolates the carrying/regrouping skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Regrouping adds a layer of complexity — must carry tens from ones product into tens computation.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure specifically identifies regrouping as the issue.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test 3-digit by 1-digit multiplication. Extension of 2x1 with added hundreds place.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Adds hundreds-place processing and potentially multiple regroupings — a natural step up.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that extending the algorithm to 3 digits is the issue.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test 4+-digit by 1-digit multiplication. Further extension.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Larger numbers require sustained algorithmic execution across more place values.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies breakdown in executing the algorithm with larger numbers.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test the area model (box method) for 2-digit by 2-digit multiplication. A student could pass this but fail the standard algorithm.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: The area model decomposes both factors and sums four partial products — a conceptual approach distinct from the standard algorithm.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Problems ask for final answers; the area model is a mental/pencil strategy.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that the student can't decompose both factors and sum partial products.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test the standard algorithm for 2-digit by 2-digit multiplication. A student could pass area model but fail the standard algorithm.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Standard algorithm requires specific procedural steps (multiply by ones digit, shift, multiply by tens digit, add) — different from area model decomposition.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to execute the standard multiplication algorithm.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test multi-digit by 2-digit multiplication (3-digit x 2-digit, etc.). Extension of 2x2 standard.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Larger first factor means more partial products and more regrouping — a fluency step up.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that extending to larger first factors is the specific gap.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test 3-digit by 3-digit multiplication. The most complex whole-number multiplication.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Three partial products, each multi-digit, with alignment — maximum algorithmic complexity for whole numbers.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies breakdown at highest multiplication complexity level.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test estimating products by rounding. Focused on multiplication estimation only.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Estimating requires rounding judgment and mental math — different from exact computation.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to estimate products, which is important for reasonableness checking.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test equal-group word problems using multiplication.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Very similar to multiply-word-problems which already covers 'equal groups, arrays, and comparison.' The equal-groups subset doesn't require a meaningfully different strategy.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: The diagnostic value vs. multiply-word-problems is marginal — if a student fails equal-group problems, they likely fail the broader word problems topic too.

multiply-word-problems-equal-groups.mergeRecommendation: Consider merging into multiply-word-problems. The parent topic already covers equal groups as one of its problem types. If kept, ensure the problem banks don't overlap.
multiply-word-problems-equal-groups.notes: The hard problems here use multi-digit multiplication (12x36), which goes beyond Grade 3 fact fluency. May be better as a feed-in to multi-step word problems.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test multiplicative comparison ('N times as many'). Distinct from equal-group word problems.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Multiplicative comparison requires understanding 'times as many' language, which is a specific comprehension challenge different from 'groups of.'

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure specifically identifies confusion with multiplicative comparison language.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems apply multiplication to compute area. Bridges multiplication with geometry.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires understanding area = length x width in real-world contexts — different from pure computation or other word problem types.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to connect multiplication with area concepts.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test understanding inverse relationship and fact families. Distinct from computing products or quotients.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires relational/structural thinking — if I know a*b=c, I know c/a=b — different from procedural computation.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Numeric and short text answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies that a student doesn't see multiplication and division as related operations.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Too narrow for standalone remediation. If a student fails this, the remediation is essentially 'review multiplication properties.'

commutative-property-mult.mergeRecommendation: Merge with properties-of-multiplication or the associative-distributive-mult topic. Individual property topics are too granular for useful diagnostics.
commutative-property-mult.notes: Very narrow topic. Better as part of a combined properties topic.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: Problems test associative and distributive properties.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Combined with commutative-property-mult and multiply-by-0-1, these fragment the properties-of-multiplication topic into pieces too small for useful diagnosis.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Numeric or conceptual answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Too narrow — remediation for associative/distributive property confusion overlaps with general properties-of-multiplication remediation.

associative-distributive-mult.mergeRecommendation: Merge with properties-of-multiplication. The combined topic already covers all three properties and provides better diagnostic value than fragmented property topics.
associative-distributive-mult.notes: See commutative-property-mult notes. These three property subtopics should consolidate.

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test division fact recall up to 100. Pure procedural fluency, parallel to multiply-within-100.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Division fact recall requires thinking 'what times divisor = dividend' — inverse reasoning from multiplication facts.

platform_compatible.reasoning: Simple numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies lack of division fact fluency — specific and actionable.

divide-within-100.notes: Capstone division facts topic, with granular children (divide-by-2-5-10, divide-by-3-4, divide-by-6-7-8-9).

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test translating word problems into division. Distinct from raw fact recall.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Requires identifying division structure (sharing vs. grouping) in context — application-level skill.

platform_compatible.reasoning: All text-based with numeric answers.

remediation_useful.reasoning: Failure identifies inability to apply division in word problem contexts.

division-word-problems.notes: More specific word problem subtopics exist (equal-sharing, how-many-groups, interpret-remainder).

testable_in_isolation.reasoning: All problems test the long division algorithm with 1-digit divisors and up to 4-digit dividends. Consistent procedural skill.

distinct_cognitive_demand.reasoning: Long division algorithm (divide-multiply-subtract-bring down) is a distinct multi-step procedure from basic division facts.
